last_excerpt: them. The inside of a golf ball is — I don't actually know anymore. I thought I knew. Rubber bands, maybe. Which would be something. Tensioned things. A bow before the arrow. A spring that's been compressed so long it's forgotten it was ever a spring. Pallets in a warehouse, wrapped in that clear stretch plastic, round and round and round. Someone walks in circles and the thing becomes one thing. Mummification is just that. A very slow version of that. No it isn't. --- There's a bird that weaves its nest so tightly it can hold water. I saw a picture once. The nest was still intact after the bird was long gone and it still held water, you could fill it like a cup. That's not a metaphor for anything, it's just what the bird did. The bird wasn't thinking about legacy. The bird was building a nest.
last_concepts: a password you remember but don't use anymore
a signature that's become a shape no one can read
the weight of an envelope you haven't opened
a driveway crack filled with tar
